modules.title: React Core Concepts

sections.heading: What is React
sections.content: React is a JavaScript library by Meta for building user interfaces using reusable components. It uses Virtual DOM to efficiently update only what changed.
sections.tip: React is a LIBRARY not framework. Angular is a framework.

sections.heading: Components and Props
sections.content: Components are reusable UI blocks. Props are read-only inputs from parent to child. One-way data flow.
sections.tip: Props are READ-ONLY. Never modify props. Use state for changing data.

sections.heading: Conditional Rendering
sections.content: Three patterns: && operator, ternary, and early return.
sections.tip: Use early return for multiple conditions. Cleaner than nested ternaries.

sections.heading: Lists and Keys
sections.content: map() renders lists. Every item needs unique key for efficient tracking.
sections.tip: Never use array index as key if list can reorder.

key_points: React is a library not framework
Props flow down and are read-only
Every list item needs unique key

module_quiz.question: React is a?
module_quiz.explanation: React is a JS library by Meta. Angular is a framework.

module_quiz.question: Props are?
module_quiz.explanation: Props are read-only inputs passed from parent to child.

module_quiz.question: Why keys in lists?
module_quiz.explanation: Keys help React identify changes for efficient re-rendering.

module_quiz.question: Index as key is?
module_quiz.explanation: Index keys break when items reorder because index changes.

module_quiz.question: className not class because?
module_quiz.explanation: class is reserved keyword for JS class declarations.

modules.title: Hooks Deep Dive

sections.heading: useState
sections.content: Adds state to function components. Returns [value, setter]. Updates trigger re-render.
sections.tip: Use functional updater (prev => prev+1) when new state depends on old state.

sections.heading: useEffect
sections.content: Runs side effects: API calls, timers, subscriptions. Replaces lifecycle methods.
sections.tip: Empty [] = mount once. With deps = run on change. No [] = every render.

sections.heading: useRef
sections.content: Mutable reference that persists without re-render. Used for DOM access.
sections.tip: useRef changes dont trigger re-render. useState changes do.

sections.heading: useContext
sections.content: Share data across components without prop drilling.
sections.tip: Dont overuse context. It re-renders ALL consumers when value changes.

key_points: useState for component state with immutable updates
useEffect for side effects with cleanup
useRef for DOM access without re-render
useContext for global state like theme and auth

module_quiz.question: useState setter triggers?
module_quiz.explanation: Setting state triggers component re-render.

module_quiz.question: useEffect([]) runs?
module_quiz.explanation: Empty deps = run once when component mounts.

module_quiz.question: useRef change triggers re-render?
module_quiz.explanation: Ref changes are silent. No re-render.

module_quiz.question: setUser({...user, name:'Alice'}) does?
module_quiz.explanation: Spread creates new object. Immutable update.

module_quiz.question: useEffect cleanup runs?
module_quiz.explanation: Cleanup prevents memory leaks.

sections.heading: useReducer
sections.content: For complex state with multiple actions. Separates logic from component.
sections.tip: useReducer when state has multiple sub-values or next state depends on previous.

sections.heading: Lifting State and Context
sections.content: Siblings share state via common parent. Context avoids prop drilling.
sections.tip: Component composition often solves prop drilling better than context.

module_quiz.question: useReducer better than useState when?
module_quiz.explanation: Shines with complex state and multiple action types.

module_quiz.question: dispatch does?
module_quiz.explanation: Sends action object to reducer function.

module_quiz.question: Lifting state means?
module_quiz.explanation: Parent holds state, passes to children as props.

module_quiz.question: Context re-renders?
module_quiz.explanation: Every useContext consumer re-renders when value changes.

module_quiz.question: Component composition?
options: Pass components as children instead of drilling props
module_quiz.explanation: Pass the needing component directly as children.

sections.heading: React Router
sections.content: SPA navigation without page reload.
sections.tip: Use Link not <a>. Link does client-side routing. <a> reloads page.

sections.heading: API Calls
sections.content: Always handle loading, error, success. Cancel on unmount.
sections.tip: AbortController cancels fetch on unmount. Prevents memory leak.

module_quiz.question: Link vs <a>?
module_quiz.explanation: Link navigates without reloading page.

module_quiz.question: useParams returns?
module_quiz.explanation: Extracts dynamic params from URL.

module_quiz.question: AbortController in useEffect?
module_quiz.explanation: Prevents setState on unmounted component.

module_quiz.question: path * means?
module_quiz.explanation: Matches unmatched URLs for 404 page.

module_quiz.question: useNavigate for?
module_quiz.explanation: Navigate from event handlers and functions.

modules.title: Performance

sections.heading: React.memo
sections.content: Prevents re-render if props unchanged. For expensive components.
sections.tip: Dont memo everything. Only expensive components with frequent same-prop renders.

sections.heading: useMemo and useCallback
sections.content: useMemo caches values. useCallback caches functions.
sections.tip: useCallback essential for function props to memo components.

sections.heading: Lazy Loading
sections.content: Load components only when needed.
sections.code:
const Dashboard = lazy(() => import('./Dashboard'));

<Suspense fallback={<Spinner />}>
    <Dashboard />
</Suspense>
sections.tip: Lazy load route-level components. Not small ones.

key_points: memo prevents re-render if props same

module_quiz.question: memo does?
module_quiz.explanation: Skips render when props are same as last time.

module_quiz.question: useMemo vs useCallback?
module_quiz.explanation: useMemo returns cached value. useCallback returns cached function.

module_quiz.question: useCallback with memo?
options: Without it new function breaks memo
module_quiz.explanation: New function reference every render makes memo useless.

module_quiz.question: lazy() does?
options: Load component only when first rendered
module_quiz.explanation: Dynamic import downloads code only when needed.

module_quiz.question: When use memo?
options: Expensive components with frequent same props
module_quiz.explanation: Only for components that render often with unchanged props.

modules.title: Interview Questions

sections.heading: Virtual DOM
sections.content: React creates JS object tree. Diffs old vs new. Updates only changed parts.
sections.tip: Virtual DOM isnt faster than manual DOM. Its faster than naive re-render everything.

sections.heading: Controlled vs Uncontrolled
sections.content: Controlled: state drives input. Uncontrolled: ref reads DOM value.
sections.tip: Controlled for most forms. Uncontrolled for file inputs.

sections.heading: Error Boundaries
sections.content: Catch errors in children. Must be class component.
sections.tip: No hook equivalent exists. Class components still needed for this.

key_points: Virtual DOM diffs for minimal updates
Uncontrolled: DOM owns value via ref
Error boundaries must be class components

module_quiz.question: Virtual DOM is?
module_quiz.explanation: Lightweight JS representation for efficient updates.

module_quiz.question: Controlled component?
module_quiz.explanation: React state is single source of truth for input.

module_quiz.question: componentDidMount in hooks?
module_quiz.explanation: Empty deps useEffect runs once on mount.

module_quiz.question: Error boundaries are?
module_quiz.explanation: getDerivedStateFromError only in class components.

module_quiz.question: Reconciliation?
options: Diffing algorithm comparing virtual DOM trees
module_quiz.explanation: Compares old and new virtual DOM for minimal updates.

modules.title: Custom Hooks

sections.heading: Building Custom Hooks
sections.content: Extract reusable stateful logic. Must start with use prefix.
sections.tip: Custom hooks share logic not state. Each component gets own copy.

sections.heading: Common Custom Hooks
sections.content: Patterns you will use in every project.
sections.tip: Check react-use and usehooks-ts before writing your own.

key_points: Each component gets own state copy

module_quiz.question: Custom hooks start with?
module_quiz.explanation: React rule: custom hooks must use 'use' prefix.

module_quiz.question: Two components same hook share state?
module_quiz.explanation: Each call gets independent state.

module_quiz.question: useDebounce for?
options: Delay rapid input to reduce API calls
module_quiz.explanation: Waits until user stops typing before calling API.

module_quiz.question: Custom hooks can call hooks?
module_quiz.explanation: Can use any hook inside custom hooks.

module_quiz.question: useLocalStorage combines?
module_quiz.explanation: State for value, effect to sync with localStorage.

sections.heading: Form Handling
sections.content: Controlled inputs with validation and error handling.
sections.tip: Computed property [name]: value handles multiple inputs with one handler.

module_quiz.question: e.preventDefault() in form?
module_quiz.explanation: Stops default form submission reload.

module_quiz.question: [name]: value is?
module_quiz.explanation: Variable name becomes the object key.

module_quiz.question: Clear errors when?
module_quiz.explanation: Better UX to clear as user corrects.

module_quiz.question: Disable submit when?
module_quiz.explanation: Prevents double submission.

module_quiz.question: Object.keys(errors).length === 0?
module_quiz.explanation: No keys means no validation errors.

sections.heading: React Testing Library
sections.content: Test behavior not implementation. Test what user sees.
sections.tip: Test WHAT user sees not HOW its implemented.

module_quiz.question: RTL philosophy?
module_quiz.explanation: Test behavior and what users see.

module_quiz.question: screen.getByText does?
module_quiz.explanation: Queries DOM for element with that text.

module_quiz.question: waitFor for?
module_quiz.explanation: Retries assertion until passes or timeout.

module_quiz.question: Mock fetch why?
module_quiz.explanation: Isolates tests from external services.

module_quiz.question: fireEvent.click does?
module_quiz.explanation: Triggers click event on element.

modules.title: Architecture and Best Practices

sections.heading: Project Structure
sections.content: Feature-based scales better than type-based.
sections.code:
// GOOD feature-based
src/
  features/
    auth/ (LoginForm, useAuth, authApi)
    dashboard/ (Dashboard, useDashboard)
  shared/
    components/ (Button, Spinner)
    hooks/ (useFetch)
sections.tip: Group by feature not file type.

sections.heading: Anti-Patterns
sections.content: Common mistakes that cause bugs.
sections.tip: If computable from existing state, dont store separately.

sections.heading: Performance Checklist
sections.content: Production React optimization steps.
sections.tip: React DevTools Profiler shows which components re-render and why.

key_points: Dont put derived values in state

module_quiz.question: Feature-based means?
module_quiz.explanation: Auth folder has LoginForm useAuth authApi together.

module_quiz.question: Derived value in state?
module_quiz.explanation: Computable values should not be stored as state.

module_quiz.question: Inline style={{}} breaks memo?
module_quiz.explanation: New object = new ref = memo thinks props changed.

module_quiz.question: react-window for?
module_quiz.explanation: Only renders visible items. 10000 items but 20 in DOM.

module_quiz.question: useEffect for?
module_quiz.explanation: Side effects only. Not events or derived values.

quiz.question: useCallback for?
options: Cache fn ref for memo children
